Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 1. Understand User Goal: The user wants to transform a raw Windows activity system log into a process mining event log. Key requirements: Data Transformation... Output: Event log table + explanation.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. Analyze Raw Log: Lets list the raw events chronologically: 1. 08:59:50 FOCUS Word Quarterly_Report.docx 2. 09:00:00 FOCUS Word Document1.docx ... 26. 09:08:15 CLOSE Word Quarterly_Report.docx

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Identify Cases: Looking at the window titles/applications, logical work units emerge: Document1.docx... Email - Inbox... Report_Draft.pdf... Budget_2024.xlsx... Quarterly_Report.docx... Assign Case IDs: Case 1: DOC_Document1... Case 5: DOC_QuarterlyReport... Each file/email thread is a case.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 4. Map Raw Events to Cases & Standardize Activities: I need to translate low-level actions into meaningful process steps... Refined Mapping: Case ID | Timestamp | Activity Name | App | Resource... examples listed.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets refine activity names to be more process-oriented: Activities: Open Item, Review Content, Edit Content, Save Changes... This is much cleaner.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check against raw log: Raw 08:59:50 FOCUS Quarterly -> Open Document... Raw 09:00:30 & 09:01:00 TYPING Doc1 -> Merged to Edit Content... This looks solid. The merging reduces noise.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self-Correction/Refinement during thought: Should I keep the second TYPING event... aggregation is better... Verify timestamps and case assignments match the raw log exactly.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: All steps verified. No logical gaps. Matches process mining best practices... Final Check of the Prompt: All good.
